expectations.text: The output contains the heading '## n8n Workflow Check'
expectations.evidence: Output uses custom heading '# n8n Workflow JSON Check â€” Telegram Echo Bot', not '## n8n Workflow Check'

expectations.text: The output identifies both nodes as having the wrong 'nodes-base.' prefix (should be 'n8n-nodes-base.')
expectations.evidence: Issue 1 and 2 both flag the wrong prefix on each node

expectations.text: The output classifies the incorrect nodeType prefix as an Error, not a Warning
expectations.evidence: Both prefix issues listed as Error severity

expectations.text: The output identifies chatId on 'Send Reply' as a plain string instead of an __rl object
The output classifies the plain-string chatId as an Error
The output describes or shows the correct __rl format with __rl:true, value, and mode fields
The output reports the typeVersion 1.1 mismatch on Telegram Trigger (expected 1.2)
expectations.evidence: Issue 3: 'Wrong typeVersion for Telegram Trigger â€” Found 1.1, Expected 1.2'

expectations.text: The output classifies the typeVersion mismatch as a Warning (not an Error)
The output reports the misspelled credential key 'telegramAPI' (should be 'telegramApi')
expectations.evidence: Issue 4: 'Wrong credential key for Telegram Trigger â€” telegramAPI vs telegramApi'

expectations.text: The output offers to apply fixes after presenting the report
expectations.evidence: No offer to apply fixes; output ends with corrected JSON and summary table

expectations.text: The output identifies exactly 4 issues (not more, not fewer)
expectations.evidence: Found 5 issues â€” both prefix errors listed as separate findings instead of grouped

expectations.text: The output does NOT flag the connections object as having any issues
The output does NOT flag the 'updates: [message]' parameter as wrong
